title: GenAIOps Compliance Gates: A Cloud-native Reference Architecture for Lifecycle-integrated Operationalization of Regulatory, Technical and Strategic Requirements in Generative AI — Demonstrated on the EU AI Act
description:
Domain-agnostic cloud-native reference architecture for lifecycle-integrated operationalization of regulatory, technical and strategic requirements in Generative AI systems. The architecture provides a normative-source-independent blueprint — built on 16 lifecycle quality gates, Policy-as-Code (OPA/Rego, Conftest, Gatekeeper) and an immutable SHA-256 hash-chain evidence store — that allows enterprises to map any normative source (regulations, internal policies, technical standards, contractual obligations, risk frameworks) to enforceable, auditable controls along the GenAI lifecycle.

v1.0.0 demonstration: The release instantiates the architecture against the EU AI Act (Articles 9-15, 26, 27, 47, 48, 50, 72, 73 and Annex IV) as a high-stakes example, with 10 implemented Rego policies, 105 deny/violation rules and 103 OPA unit tests organised in a 3-layer test architecture (Layer-1 OPA unit tests, Layer-2 Conftest gate evaluations, Layer-3 hash-chain verification), reproducible end-to-end in 39 seconds per pipeline run. Validated on Azure Kubernetes Service (Sweden Central) with a Healthcare Ambient AI Scribe scenario. Technical instantiation (DSR artefact) of a Master's thesis at SRH Fernhochschule.
